taskSuccess.evidence: Actual MCP Client + StdioClientTransport generated production 1:1 and group results with incoming/outgoing mixed text+image content.

visualFidelity.evidence: Committed production previews show both 320x180 synthetic attachments bounded in WhatsApp bubbles, both exact texts/times, and repeated mockscreenshots.com watermark without clipping.

workflowUsability.evidence: One optional image object per message; image-only and mixed messages supported; existing text-only request remains unchanged.

speed.evidence: Production stdio calls completed in 302 ms (cached 1:1) and 2915 ms (group cold render); full local suite completed in under 4 seconds.

robustness.evidence: Tests cover missing/malformed/spoofed/oversize/SVG/non-WhatsApp inputs, 8,000-character endpoint limit, failures, timeout, MIME/signature validation, and preview response bounds.

accessibility.evidence: Attachment alt text is accepted, trimmed, capped at 160 characters, and defaults to Attached image, matching the site contract.

outputExport.evidence: Production returns inline PNG plus stable hosted full-resolution and edit URLs; both committed previews visibly retain the repeated mandatory watermark.

mcpParity.evidence: Public MCP schema emits the merged optional m[].im contract for WhatsApp and whatsapp-group; actual stdio handshake and both production render paths passed.

commands.productionProtocol: node docs/pr-evidence/whatsapp-image-attachments/capture-production.mjs (2/2 passed after one transient initial whatsapp-group render 500; immediate retry passed)
commands.packageDryRun: npm pack --dry-run (recorded in PR after final verification)

transientObservation: The first production group cold-render attempt returned HTTP 500 and correctly fell back to watermarked hosted/edit links; immediate rerun returned inline PNG+text. This is documented as an endpoint operational risk, not hidden.
